summary: Add `spec_features(&Spec) -> Features` to extract.rs, mirroring `rule_features`: id = name, `body_lines`, ATX `headings`, `source_dir`; no fields, no companions.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

edit.description: Add `pub fn spec_features(spec: &Spec) -> Features` mirroring `rule_features` — id from `spec.name`, `body_lines` from `spec.body.lines().count()`, `headings` via the existing `body_headings`, `source_dir` via `source_dir_name(&spec.provenance.source_path)`, empty `fields`/`companions`. Import `crate::spec::Spec`.

tests.asserts: `spec_features` exposes the spec id (file stem), body line count, ATX headings (skipping fenced `#`), and `source_dir`, with empty `fields` and `companions`.

acceptance: `spec_features` projects a `Spec` into `Features` carrying its name, body line count, ATX headings, and source dir (no frontmatter fields, no companions) — the read-side the spec contract will validate.
notes: Split out of SPEC-KIND-GATE: this extraction half is pure and fork-free (reuses `body_headings`/`source_dir_name`). The GATE's `check` dispatch + the `contracts/spec.toml` embed stay parked on a human committing that untracked file.

summary: Dispatch `check` over specs: embed `contracts/spec.toml`, project `ws.specs` via `spec_features`, and run admissibility + `engine::validate` (the advisory `max_lines` clause) over the spec features.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

gate.reason: the untracked `contracts/spec.toml` is curated human territory (build embeds it via `include_str!` but never writes/adds it); a human must commit it before the gate can embed it in a clean commit

edit.description: Add `BUILTIN_SPEC_CONTRACT = include_str!("../contracts/spec.toml")`; in the `Check` arm project `ws.specs` to spec features via `extract::spec_features`, parse the spec contract, and extend the diagnostic stream with `engine::admissibility` + `engine::validate` over them.

tests.asserts: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — the over-length specs trip `max_lines` at advisory severity and the run still exits 0 absent `--deny-advisories`.

acceptance: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — flagging the over-length specs at advisory severity and exiting 0 (non-blocking) absent `--deny-advisories`.
notes: Build-order: needs SPEC-FEATURES (the extractor) on disk first, plus a human to commit the untracked `contracts/spec.toml`. Runs only spec.toml's active `max_lines` clause; its two commented clauses (decisions-name-alternatives, references-resolve) await `(decision-marker-predicate)` and the references-resolve frontier — both also downstream of that spec.toml commit.
